title: Resources
text:
Recommended Reading

Textbooks

Nowak, M.A. Evolutionary Dynamics. Harvard University Press, 2006.
Rice, S.H. Evolutionary Theory: Mathematical and Conceptual Foundations. Sinauer Associates, 2004.
Hofbauer, J. & Sigmund, K. Evolutionary Games and Population Dynamics. Cambridge University Press, 1998.
Ewens, W.J. Mathematical Population Genetics. Springer, 2004.



Review Articles

Hamilton, W.D. (1964). The genetical evolution of social behaviour. Journal of Theoretical Biology, 7(1), 1–16.
Price, G.R. (1970). Selection and covariance. Nature, 227, 520–521.
Maynard Smith, J. & Szathmáry, E. (1995). The Major Transitions in Evolution. Freeman.

title: Pichugin Lab
text:
Understanding evolutionary dynamics through mathematical modeling and computation
Learn More About Us View Publications

title: Pichugin Lab
text:
Research Overview
Our laboratory investigates the evolutionary and ecological processes that shape biological diversity. We combine theoretical frameworks with computational approaches to develop models that explain population dynamics, cooperation, and the emergence of complex traits.


Evolutionary Theory
We develop and analyze mathematical models of natural selection, genetic drift, and eco-evolutionary dynamics.


Computational Modeling
We build simulation frameworks to explore the emergence of cooperation, altruism, and social behavior.


Open Science
We make our code, data, and analyses publicly available to support reproducible research. Explore our GitHub organization.

title: Pichugin Lab
text:

2025 — New preprint on multilevel selection dynamics posted to bioRxiv.
2024 — Lab GitHub organization launched. Repositories for ongoing projects are now publicly accessible.
2024 — New resources and tutorial materials added to the Resources page.

title: Pichugin Lab
text:
Join the Lab
We welcome motivated students and postdocs interested in evolutionary theory, mathematical biology, and computational modeling. See the About page for contact information and open positions.

title: Projects
text: Below are the active and completed research projects from the Pichugin Lab. Code repositories for each project are hosted in our GitHub organization.

title: Projects
text:
Active Projects

Multilevel Selection Dynamics
We are developing a general mathematical framework for multilevel selection that accounts for group formation, reproduction, and dissolution. The goal is to identify conditions under which group-level selection can drive the evolution of cooperation and novel collective-level traits.


Status: Active
Collaborators: [Names]


 Repository  Preprint





Eco-Evolutionary Feedback Models
This project studies how ecological interactions (competition, predation, mutualism) feed back into evolutionary dynamics and vice versa. We build and analyse ordinary and stochastic differential equation models to understand long-term evolutionary trajectories.


Status: Active
Collaborators: [Names]


 Repository

title: Projects
text:
Completed Projects

Evolution of Life-History Traits
A theoretical investigation of the trade-offs between growth rate, fecundity, and survival that shape life-history strategies across biological taxa. Published results are available via the Publications page.


Status: Completed


 Publication  Repository

title: Projects
text:
Linking External Repositories
This lab website is designed to link directly to other repositories within the Pichugin-Lab GitHub organization. As new repositories are made public they will be featured here with descriptions, documentation links, and usage examples.

title: About
text:
A. Pichugin, PhD
Department of [Your Department], [Your Institution]
I am a researcher studying evolutionary dynamics, cooperation, and the mathematical principles underlying biological complexity. My work draws on tools from dynamical systems, probability theory, and computer simulation to address questions about how life evolves and organizes at multiple scales.

title: About
text:
Research Interests

Mathematical modeling of evolutionary processes
Multilevel selection and major evolutionary transitions
Cooperation and the evolution of social behavior
Population genetics and eco-evolutionary dynamics
Computational approaches to theoretical biology

Open Positions
We are currently recruiting:

Postdoctoral researchers with experience in evolutionary theory or computational biology.

To apply, please send a CV, a brief statement of research interests, and contact information for two references to the email address below.
